item.title: Cost of living, jobs and affordable education top youth Budget priorities - former Youth Parliamentarian
item.description: Former Youth Parliamentarian Patricia Subbaiya rates her confidence in the upcoming National Budget at 5 out of 10, saying young people want to see action rather than more promises. Insert: Subbaiya on 5% rating 23rd June 26
item.content:encoded:
Former Youth Parliamentarian Patricia Subbaiya rates her confidence in the upcoming National Budget at 5 out of 10, saying young people want to see action rather than more promises.

Insert: Subbaiya on 5% rating 23rd June 26

Speaking to fijivillage News, Subbaiya says the biggest issues she wants addressed in the budget are the rising cost of living, job opportunities and affordable education.

She says higher food prices, transportation costs and utility bills have made it harder for families to save, with some being forced to cut back on necessities.

Subbaiya says while there have been many consultations and discussions ahead of the budget, the concerns raised by young people are not always reflected in the final outcome.

She is calling for more scholarships and increased funding for schools, resources and educational materials.

The former Youth Parliamentarian says one of the biggest challenges facing young people looking for work is the lack of experience, coupled with limited job opportunities and strong competition in the job market.

She supports increased funding for youth entrepreneurs and small businesses, saying easier access to start-up loans would encourage more young people to pursue business opportunities.

Subbaiya believes the government should invest more in tourism, information technology, agriculture and renewable energy to create employment opportunities.

She is also advocating for tax reductions and subsidies to assist young people with transportation, basic goods and medical expenses.

On housing, Subbaiya says affordable housing is a major issue due to high rental costs and limited housing availability in Fiji.

She is also calling for increased funding for mental health services, saying mental health plays a vital role in a person's ability to function in society.

Subbaiya says youth unemployment, mental health, drug abuse and HIV are among the key social issues that need greater attention in the budget.

If given the opportunity to question the Finance Minister, she says she would ask what specific measures are being introduced to ensure decent-paying jobs for young people and how the government plans to prove those initiatives are benefiting youths.

She would also ask when affordable housing will become a realistic option for young people rather than remaining a policy promise.

Subbaiya says young people will become more hopeful when they see policies translated into action and tangible results delivered.

The 2026/2027 National Budget will be delivered at 10am this Friday.

item.title: Watch fijivillage Straight Talk with Vijay Narayan on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, was held at 7pm today at the Fiji Showcase 2026, at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show were led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, was held at 7pm today at the Fiji Showcase 2026, at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show were led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

A town-hall was set up for the live show where members of the audience also participated in the discussion.

If your question is selected to be asked on the show, you have to be a Fiji Showcase 2026 patron on Wednesday evening, to ask the question in person.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Gilbert Vakalalabure and Litia Senibulu sacked, CID to look into audit report
item.description: Fiji Sports Council CEO, Gilbert Vakalalabure and Manager of Operations, Litia Senibulu have been sacked by the Council's board.Sp

item.title: Escaped man under investigation for $20M tax fraud is a naturalised Fijian citizen
item.description: Rashika Kumar , Vijay Narayan · 25/06/2026 The man who is under Fiji Revenue and Customs Service investigation for alleged tax defraud of over $20 million and fled the country, is a naturalized Fijian citizen.
item.content:encoded:
Rashika Kumar , Vijay Narayan · 25/06/2026

The man who is under Fiji Revenue and Customs Service investigation for alleged tax defraud of over $20 million and fled the country, is a naturalized Fijian citizen.

FICAC is investigating how the man who is also a Chinese national, left the country despite a Stop Departure Order against him.

FRCS is yet to confirm when the order was issued against the man.

They are also yet to confirm if the order was cancelled, and why the investigation has not concluded until now.

Minister for Immigration Viliame Naupoto has confirmed to fijivillage News that the matter is with FICAC.

He says they are working with FICAC and providing the information required for the investigation.

Naupoto has also confirmed that an internal investigation is underway at the Ministry of Immigration as FICAC's investigations continue.

Meanwhile, FICAC says it has has never issued a Stop Departure Order against the Chinese national.

item.title: 558 new domestic violence cases recorded in early 2026 - FWCC
item.description: Violence against women, girls and children continues at alarming levels across Fiji, with hundreds of new cases and thousands of repeat counselling sessions recorded in the first five months of 2026. Statistics from counselling services between January and May show 829 new cases were received, with domestic violence accounting for 558 of those reports, making it the most common form of abuse recor
item.content:encoded:
Violence against women, girls and children continues at alarming levels across Fiji, with hundreds of new cases and thousands of repeat counselling sessions recorded in the first five months of 2026.

Statistics from counselling services between January and May show 829 new cases were received, with domestic violence accounting for 558 of those reports, making it the most common form of abuse recorded.

Survivors of domestic violence also attended 1,023 repeat counselling sessions during the same period, underscoring the ongoing trauma and long-term support needed for victims.

The data further reveals cases of rape, attempted rape, sexual assault, child rape, child abuse, sexual harassment and other forms of violence affecting women and children across the country.

Among the recorded cases were 7 rape cases, 9 child rape cases, 10 cases of child sexual abuse, 6 sexual assault cases, 2 attempted rape cases, 5 sexual harassment cases, and 36 cases of physical, verbal and emotional child abuse.

Fiji Women's Crisis Centre Coordinator Shamima Ali says the figures reflect the scale of violence being experienced in homes and communities, and the sustained impact on survivors.

She says behind every statistic is a survivor seeking safety, justice and support, while the high number of repeat counselling sessions highlights that recovery from violence is a long and ongoing process.

Ali says domestic violence remains one of the most pervasive human rights violations in Fiji, with many women and children living in fear and trauma within their own homes.

She says survivors continue to face significant barriers when seeking protection and justice, including concerns over the enforcement of Domestic Violence Restraining Orders.

Ali says reports continue to be received from women who hold restraining orders but remain fearful due to delays in enforcement, adding that such protection orders are only effective if they are properly implemented.

She says domestic violence must be treated as a serious crime rather than a private family matter, warning that delays in responding to complaints or breaches can place survivors and their children at further risk.

Ali also raised concerns about the handling of domestic violence cases by authorities, saying some survivors report being discouraged from pursuing complaints, being encouraged to reconcile with perpetrators, or having their experiences minimised.

She says such responses amount to re-victimisation and undermine trust in the justice system.

Ali is calling for stronger accountability across institutions, improved police responses, and greater investment in prevention, protection and survivor support services.

She says ending violence against women requires a coordinated national response involving government agencies, law enforcement, communities and faith-based organisations.

The Fiji Women’s Crisis Centre say they remain committed to providing confidential counselling, advocacy and support services to survivors across Fiji.

The National Domestic Violence Helpline is 1560, and is available 24 hours a day free of charge from all networks.

item.title: Fuel assistance from Govt brings relief to Narikoso Village
item.description: Kelepi Taqa, a youth representative from Narikoso Village in Ono, Kadavu, expressed his gratitude and emotion after receiving assistance from the Ministry of Youth and Sports in the form of seven 44-litre drums of fuel for the village’s outboard motors. Speaking to fijivillage News, Taqa says the recent increase in fuel prices has placed a significant burden on the people of Narikoso, whose primar
item.content:encoded:
Kelepi Taqa, a youth representative from Narikoso Village in Ono, Kadavu, expressed his gratitude and emotion after receiving assistance from the Ministry of Youth and Sports in the form of seven 44-litre drums of fuel for the village’s outboard motors.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Taqa says the recent increase in fuel prices has placed a significant burden on the people of Narikoso, whose primary mode of transportation is by boat.

He explained that a trip to Kavala Port, which previously cost around $50, now costs more than $100 due to rising fuel expenses.

Taqa noted that the government’s assistance has provided much-needed relief, helping the community maintain access to essential supplies and ensuring that children can continue travelling to school.

20 recipients today received assistance from the Ministry of Youth and Sports valued at $40,000.

Minister Jese Saukuru says the Ministry is elated to help small business owners to get this assistance and reminded them to help move to promote economic sustainabilty.

item.title: Tudravu asks for increased funding to combat evolving crime and repair ageing infrastructure
item.description: With the criminal landscape continuously evolving and the need to repair ageing infrastructure, the Fiji Police Force has made submissions to the Ministry of Finance for an increase in budget allocation for the 2026-2027 financial year. While responding to questions from fijivillage News, Commissioner of Police Rusiate Tudravu says they made a submission following a review of operational efforts t
item.content:encoded:
With the criminal landscape continuously evolving and the need to repair ageing infrastructure, the Fiji Police Force has made submissions to the Ministry of Finance for an increase in budget allocation for the 2026-2027 financial year.

While responding to questions from fijivillage News, Commissioner of Police Rusiate Tudravu says they made a submission following a review of operational efforts this year and projection forecast for the next 6 to 12 months, including the need to repair ageing infrastructure.

He says the criminal landscape is continuously evolving, and this has been factored into the request for increased funding .

The Fiji Police Force was provided an increased funding of $240.3 million in the 2025-2026 National Budget, an increase of $13.5 million from the previous financial year.

The Government had approved increasing the Fiji Police Force by 1,000 officers in two phases at a cost of about $40 million while an additional $8.6 million was also been approved for police salary progression, which is expected to continue.

The 2026-2027 National Budget will be announced at 10am Friday.

item.title: Man killed after vehicle veers off road and crashes into Sawani bus shelter
item.description: A man in his 30s who was sitting at a bus stop in Sawani has become the country's latest road fatality victim after a vehicle veer
